title_jp: 莽聧潞莽楼颅 23 芒聙聰 莽拢篓茫聛聧茫聛庐氓聟聢茫聛赂
title_en: Dassai 23 芒聙聰 Beyond the Standard
subtitle: The benchmark for modern daiginjo, polished to 23%.
body:
Dassai 23 is the flagship of Asahi Shuzo in Yamaguchi, made with Yamada Nishiki rice polished down to 23%. The result is an intensely fragrant sake with notes of apple, banana, and lychee, yet a crisp, clean finish.

This sake revolutionised the industry by proving that high polishing could produce a consistent, fruit-forward style that appeals globally. It is best enjoyed slightly chilled.

title_jp: 氓聟芦忙碌路氓卤卤 莽聣鹿氓聢楼莽麓聰莽卤鲁
sake_name_jp: 氓聟芦忙碌路氓卤卤 莽聣鹿氓聢楼莽麓聰莽卤鲁
tasting_notes: Crisp and dry with a subtle rice aroma. The flavour is clean, with hints of melon and a touch of almond. Medium-bodied with a sharp, refreshing finish. Excellent as a table sake.

title_jp: 氓聧聛氓聸聸盲禄拢 芒聙聰 猫聡鲁茅芦聵茫聛庐盲赂聙忙聺炉
title_en: Juyondai 芒聙聰 The Cult Classic
subtitle: Yamagata芒聙聶s legendary sake that redefined premium brewing.
body:
Juyondai, brewed by Takagi Shuzo, is one of the most sought-after sake brands in Japan. Its rich, fruity profile and velvety texture come from the use of rare yeast strains and meticulous brewing techniques.

The signature label, "Juyondai" (氓聧聛氓聸聸盲禄拢), refers to the 14th generation of the Takagi family. This sake often features notes of melon, pear, and white flowers, with a long, elegant finish.

title_jp: 氓陇漏莽聥聴猫聢聻 氓卤卤氓禄聝莽麓聰莽卤鲁
sake_name_jp: 氓陇漏莽聥聴猫聢聻 氓卤卤氓禄聝莽麓聰莽卤鲁
tasting_notes: A bold, savoury sake with aromas of roasted nuts, yoghurt, and a hint of mushroom. On the palate, it芒聙聶s full-bodied with a sharp acidity that cuts through fatty fish. The finish is long and dry.

title_jp: 盲鹿聟盲驴聺莽聰掳 猫聬卢氓炉驴 芒聙聰 忙聳掳忙陆聼茫聛庐忙掳聴氓聯聛
title_en: Kubota Manju 芒聙聰 The Elegance of Niigata
subtitle: A flagship sake from the heart of Niigata, known for its clean, crisp profile.
body:
Kubota Manju is the pinnacle of Asahi Shuzo芒聙聶s brewing, representing the tanrei karakuchi style that Niigata is famous for. With a light, dry character and subtle fruit notes, it pairs perfectly with delicate seafood.

This sake is brewed using Gohyakumangoku rice, polished to 50%, and fermented at low temperatures to develop its refined aroma. The name "Manju" (猫聬卢氓炉驴) means "ten thousand longevities," symbolizing a wish for enduring happiness.

title_jp: 猫聫聤忙掳麓 莽麓聰莽卤鲁氓聬聼茅聠赂
sake_name_jp: 猫聫聤忙掳麓 莽麓聰莽卤鲁氓聬聼茅聠赂
tasting_notes: Light and refreshing with subtle aromas of green apple and white flowers. The palate is smooth with a gentle sweetness balanced by a crisp, clean finish. A textbook Niigata ginjo.

title_jp: 莽聰聼茅聟聸茫聛庐忙聤聙忙鲁聲 芒聙聰 盲录聺莽碌卤茫聜聮茅聠赂茫聛聶
subtitle: A labour-intensive method that yields complex, earthy flavours.
body:
Kimoto is the traditional yeast-starter method that predates modern additions of lactic acid. Brewers literally grind the rice mixture with poles to encourage wild lactic acid bacteria, a process that takes weeks but results in sake with deep umami and a robust character.

Today, only a handful of breweries still use the full kimoto method, valuing its ability to create distinctive, age-worthy sake.

title_jp: 氓卤卤氓禄聝茫聛庐盲赂聳莽聲聦 芒聙聰 茅聡聨莽聰聼茫聛庐忙聛炉氓聬鹿
subtitle: The rustic, wild cousin of kimoto.
body:
Yamahai (氓卤卤氓禄聝) is a shortened form of yama-oroshi haishi, a method that skips the labour-intensive grinding of kimoto but still relies on natural lactic acid. It produces sake with pronounced acidity, gamey notes, and a full-bodied texture.

Once seen as difficult to control, yamahai is now celebrated for the complexity it brings to junmai and ginjo styles.
